facts: Alice works on the payments team
The payments team owns the Stripe integration
query: Who maintains the Stripe integration?

facts: The API runs on port 8080
Port 8080 is load-balanced by nginx
query: What load balances the API?

facts: Bob manages the Kubernetes cluster
The Kubernetes cluster runs on AWS EKS
query: Who manages our EKS deployment?

facts: The frontend uses Next.js
Next.js is deployed on Vercel
query: Where is the frontend hosted?

facts: Carol wrote the auth service
The auth service uses JWT tokens
query: Who implemented JWT authentication?

facts: The ML pipeline runs nightly
Nightly jobs use spot instances
query: What compute does the ML pipeline use?

facts: Tests run in CI
CI uses GitHub Actions
query: Where do tests execute?

facts: The search service uses Elasticsearch
Elasticsearch runs on 3 dedicated nodes
query: How many nodes power search?

facts: Dave is the security lead
The security lead approves all production access requests
query: Who approves production access?

facts: Error tracking uses Sentry
Sentry alerts go to the #incidents Slack channel
query: Where do error alerts appear?

facts: The database is PostgreSQL
PostgreSQL backups go to S3
query: Where are database backups stored?

facts: The mobile app is built with React Native
React Native builds are handled by Fastlane
query: What tool builds the mobile app?

facts: Feature flags use LaunchDarkly
LaunchDarkly config is cached for 5 minutes
query: How long until a feature flag change takes effect?

facts: The logging stack uses Loki
Loki data is retained for 30 days
query: How far back can we search logs?

facts: DNS is on Route 53
Route 53 has health checks every 10 seconds
query: How often are DNS health checks run?

facts: The recommendation engine was built by the ML team
The ML team uses Python 3.12
Python 3.12 requires the latest NumPy 2.0
query: What NumPy version does the recommendation engine need?

facts: Payments are processed by the billing service
The billing service stores data in the transactions database
The transactions database has row-level encryption
query: How is payment data encrypted at rest?
gold_answer: Row-level encryption in the transactions database

facts: User uploads go to the media service
The media service runs in the eu-west-1 region
EU regions are subject to GDPR data residency requirements
query: What compliance requirements apply to user uploads?

facts: The notification service sends push notifications
Push notifications use Firebase Cloud Messaging
FCM has a rate limit of 1000 messages per second
query: What's the throughput limit for push notifications?

facts: Alice leads the platform team
The platform team owns the API gateway
The API gateway handles rate limiting
query: Whose team is responsible for rate limiting?

facts: Staging mirrors production
Production uses m5.xlarge instances
m5.xlarge has 4 vCPUs and 16 GB RAM
query: How much RAM does each staging instance have?

facts: The data pipeline writes to BigQuery
BigQuery tables are partitioned by ingestion date
Partitioned tables auto-expire after 90 days
query: How long is pipeline data retained in the analytics warehouse?

facts: Carol manages the CI/CD pipeline
The CI pipeline runs on GitHub Actions
GitHub Actions runners are self-hosted on ARM64 machines
query: What architecture are Carol's CI runners?

facts: The checkout flow calls the pricing service
The pricing service reads from a Redis cache
The Redis cache TTL for prices is 60 seconds
query: How stale can checkout prices be?

facts: Microservices communicate via gRPC
gRPC uses HTTP/2 transport
The load balancer doesn't support HTTP/2 natively
query: What transport compatibility issue exists with the load balancer?
gold_answer: Load balancer doesn't support HTTP/2 (needed by gRPC)

facts: The auth service issues JWT tokens
JWT tokens expire after 15 minutes
Refresh tokens are stored in HttpOnly cookies
query: Where are refresh tokens kept and how long do access tokens last?
gold_answer: Refresh tokens in HttpOnly cookies, access tokens expire in 15 minutes

facts: Bob is on call this week
On-call engineers get paged via PagerDuty
PagerDuty escalation timeout is 10 minutes
query: If Bob doesn't respond to an alert, how long before it escalates?

facts: Static assets are served by CloudFront
CloudFront origins point to an S3 bucket
The S3 bucket has versioning enabled
query: Can we roll back a bad static asset deployment?
gold_answer: Yes, S3 versioning is enabled on the CloudFront origin bucket

facts: The search service indexes product data
Product data is sourced from the catalog service
The catalog service publishes change events to Kafka
query: How does search know when products change?
gold_answer: Via Kafka events from the catalog service

facts: Database migrations use Alembic
Alembic is configured with async PostgreSQL driver
The async driver is asyncpg
query: What PostgreSQL driver handles schema migrations?

facts: The primary database is in us-east-1
Read replicas are in us-west-2 and eu-west-1
EU users are routed to the nearest region
GDPR requires EU data to stay in EU
query: Can EU user data be read from the us-west-2 replica?
gold_answer: No — EU users route to eu-west-1 for GDPR compliance

facts: Service A depends on Service B
Service B depends on Service C
Service C has a 200ms P99 latency
Each service adds 50ms overhead
query: What's the expected P99 latency for a request hitting Service A?
gold_answer: 300ms (200ms from C + 50ms each for B and A)

facts: The ML model requires GPU for inference
GPU instances cost $3/hour
The model serves 1000 requests per second
Each request takes 10ms of GPU time
query: What's the minimum GPU utilization for the ML model?
gold_answer: 10 seconds of GPU per second (1000 * 10ms), so 10 GPUs minimum

facts: Kafka topic retention is 7 days
The consumer lag alert triggers at 1 hour
The dead letter queue holds failed messages for 30 days
Message processing timeout is 30 seconds
query: If a consumer is down for 2 days, can messages still be recovered?
gold_answer: Yes — Kafka retains for 7 days, well within the 2-day window

facts: Deployments use blue-green strategy
Blue-green switch happens after health checks pass
Health checks run every 30 seconds for 5 minutes
Rollback is automatic if error rate exceeds 1%
query: How many health check cycles must pass before traffic switches?

facts: The ETL pipeline runs at 2 AM UTC
ETL depends on the data warehouse being idle
The data warehouse runs analytics queries from 6 AM to 10 PM UTC
ETL takes 3 hours to complete
query: Is there a conflict between ETL and analytics workloads?
gold_answer: Yes — ETL runs 2-5 AM but analytics start at 6 AM, tight but no overlap

facts: The connection pool has 50 connections per instance
There are 10 service instances
PostgreSQL max_connections is set to 200
PgBouncer multiplexes connections
query: Would all instances saturate the database without PgBouncer?
gold_answer: Yes — 50 * 10 = 500 exceeds the 200 max_connections limit; PgBouncer is required

facts: Secrets rotate every 90 days
The last rotation was 85 days ago
Rotation requires a deploy to pick up new credentials
Deploys are frozen during the holiday code freeze
query: Is there a risk of credential expiry during the code freeze?
gold_answer: Yes — secrets expire in 5 days but deploys are frozen

facts: API rate limit is 100 req/min per user
The batch import endpoint counts as 1 request but processes 1000 items
Enterprise users have a 10x rate limit multiplier
query: How many items can an enterprise user batch-import per minute?
gold_answer: 1,000,000 items (1000 rate limit * 1000 items per request)

facts: CDN cache TTL is 24 hours
Cache invalidation takes 5 minutes
The marketing team updates the landing page daily at 9 AM
Users in Asia see the page first each day due to timezone
query: When do Asian users see updated landing page content?
gold_answer: By 9:05 AM (9 AM update + 5 min cache invalidation)

facts: The circuit breaker opens at 50% error rate
Service B has been returning 503 for 40% of requests
Service A calls Service B for user profiles
There's a cached fallback for user profiles that's 1 hour stale
query: Is the circuit breaker to Service B currently open?
gold_answer: No — 40% is below the 50% threshold, but it's close

facts: The S3 bucket uses server-side encryption with AWS KMS
KMS key rotation happens annually
The compliance team requires encryption key rotation every 90 days
AWS KMS automatic rotation creates new key material yearly
query: Does our S3 encryption meet the compliance rotation requirement?
gold_answer: No — KMS rotates annually but compliance requires 90-day rotation

facts: Elasticsearch cluster has 3 data nodes
Each node has 64 GB RAM
ES recommends 50% of RAM for heap
The index size is 500 GB across all shards
query: How much heap memory is available across the ES cluster?

facts: The async worker pool has 20 workers
Each worker processes one job at a time
Average job duration is 30 seconds
Peak queue depth reaches 600 jobs at noon
query: How long would the peak queue take to clear with no new jobs?

facts: GraphQL queries have a depth limit of 5
The user -> orders -> items -> product -> reviews chain is depth 4
Reviews have a nested author field
query: Can a query fetch review authors through the full order chain?
gold_answer: No — that would be depth 5 (user->orders->items->product->reviews->author = 6), exceeding the limit
